Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Declaration Of Sunnatillo Muhamadiev
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Advanced From Mar-09-18 To Mar-01-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Declaration Of Sunnatillo Muhamadiev
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sunnatillo Muhamadiev, 415-361-3195 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed.

The Court inquires if Defendant is aware of the tentative ruling. The Court explains the tentative ruling re: filing of a response/answer-general denial/document by 3/21/18, also a certified copy needs to be sent to the DMV. The Court is giving a 2nd pass to Defendant. It will not happen again. The Court suggests the Access Center or the Law Library. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUNNATILLO MUHAMADIEV's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment.

Defendant Sunnatillo Muhamadiev's motion to set aside the default of May 30, 2017 and the default judgment of July 5, 2017 is granted. Based on Mr. Muhamadiev's statements and presentation in the courtroom on March 1, 2018, the court is persuaded that Mr. Muhamadiev was unaware that he was required to file a response to the complaint by May 25, 2017 and he did not realize that a default and default judgment had been entered against him until he recently learned that his driver's license was suspended due to his failure to satisfy the default judgment. Per applicable law, under normal circumstances Mr. Muhamadiev's mistakes in failing to file a response to the complaint and failing to file his motion to set aside the default and default judgment within the six month period of CCP 473(b) would mandate the denial of this motion, which was the court's tentative ruling prior to the March 1, 2018 hearing. However, because this court finds that Mr. Muhamadiev had no understanding that he made these mistakes and the consequence of not granting this motion would result in Mr. Muhamadiev losing his ability to lawfully drive a vehicle without a hearing on the merits of State Farm's claim notwithstanding Mr. Muhamadiev's persistent contention that he is not liable for any of the damages sought by State Farm, the court has determined that the combination of equitable relief based on extrinsic mistake and due process rights to a hearing regarding the loss of a driver's license affords this court the discretion to grant this motion and to give Mr. Muhamadiev one final opportunity to litigate this case on the merits. The default and default judgment are set aside and Mr. Muhamadiev is given leave to file an answer or other response to the complaint no later than March 21, 2018.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) A copy and a certified copy of the order is given to the Defendant.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
